explanatory_strength.rationale: The packet ties durable economics to a specific reinvestment mechanism: AWS scale and margin (Q1 2026 sales +28%, 37.7% segment OI margin), retail logistics density, and high-margin ads/silicon embedded in segment growth. The distinguishing test is whether AI capex converts to normalized owner cash rather than permanent capital consumption; that test is stated with falsifiers, not generic growth narrative.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Core operating facts reconcile from SEC extracts (TTM OCF $148.5B, productive-asset capex $151.0B, Q1 sales $181.5B, cash $86.8B). Decision-grade gaps remain: sustainable capex (~$90B) and diluted share count are [Assumption]/[HUMAN REVIEW], and the routed proof row labels raw operating cash flow ($7.695B) as normalized owner earnings instead of the filing-grounded $58.5B / ~$5.35 per share bridge in evidence reconciliation.

downside_control.rationale: Balance-sheet liquidity and massive operating cash flow limit insolvency risk and provide a real economic floor. Downside is not structurally protected at today's price: trailing FCF is ~$1.2B, capex nearly equals OCF, and equity remains exposed to prolonged AI build-out and multiple compression on ~46x normalized owner cash.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Evident base synthesis is 3.68% per year at ~$247-250 on $5.35/sh normalized owner cash; Lawrence gate is 1.9%. Bull scenario is 7.2% and contract reinvestment DCF base implies deeply negative annualized return at spot. None of the filing-supported paths clears the Buffett/Weschler 12% hurdle without unproven AI inflection assumptions ($9/sh, 16.2% sensitivity only).

claims.claim: Amazon is a widening-scale compounder with stable moat characteristics (AWS lock-in, Prime, logistics, custom silicon) and filing-supported operating momentum, squarely within the quality_reinvestment power zone.

Normalized owner cash (~$5.35/sh from $148.5B OCF less ~$90B sustainable capex) is the correct owner-earnings anchor, not trailing $1.2B FCF or the contract proof row that uses unadjusted operating cash flow.
At ~$250 per share, evidenced return paths (1.9%-7.2%) fail the 12% owner-earnings reinvestment bar; capital deployment is not approvable on base economics despite franchise quality.

strongest_counter_explanation: The AI infrastructure capex cycle is temporary capital allocation, not permanent value destruction: AWS re-acceleration, ads scale above $70B TTM, and custom silicon will lift normalized owner earnings toward $9+ per share within three to four years, making today's price fair for a widening-moat compounder priced on future distributable cash.
most_important_missing_fact: Management-disclosed sustainable capex run rate and explicit timeline for free cash flow normalization after the AI data-center build cycle (including whether ~$90B mid-cycle capex is achievable without sacrificing AWS share).
falsifiers: Two consecutive quarters with operating cash flow growth below 5% year over year while productive-asset capex stays above $140B.
AWS revenue growth decelerates below 15% with segment operating margin falling below 32% for two consecutive quarters.
Normalized owner cash per share fails to exceed $6 by 2028 while share count dilution exceeds 2% per year.
specialist_findings: Within power zone (compounder, stable moat, partial dhando). Audited the owner_earnings_reinvestment_dcf proof: reinvestment rate (20-50%), incremental ROIC (12-25%), discount rate, and terminal multiple are bounded but the owner_earnings input is mis-specified as FY2025 operating cash flow rather than normalized owner earnings, producing contract value_per_share ($6-$23) far below spot and annualized returns of -41% to -29% at $249.99. No comparable set is attached. Single additive component avoids overlap double-count; advertising and AI backlog remain embedded_in_segment with explicit zero/milestone treatments. Overlap controls pass; capital requirement risk is the capex-OCF gap (~$61B reserve), not balance-sheet distress.
